behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed; unchanged files are skipped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (PR changed-file discovery -> analysis target selection), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real network/OS; an in-process fake changed-file provider can reproduce selection bugs. MOCK-HIDE: mocking too deep (returning already-filtered files) would hide bugs, but wiring real selector + analyzer with fake PR file list catches them. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior only states 'Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed' and does not require real GitHub API/runner behavior to expose the core filtering defect.
behaviors.test_description: Wire changed-file collector, file enumerator, and analysis scheduler together in-process. Feed a mixed repo fixture plus a fake PR changed-files set; assert only changed paths are scheduled/analyzed and unchanged paths are never passed to analyzer.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: grouping correctness is exposed when findings model, formatter, and comment payload builder interact. REAL-INFRA: real GitHub HTTP call is not required to catch mis-grouping; capture outgoing payload in-process. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing only final rendered comment string too aggressively can hide mapping bugs; using real grouping + templating modules avoids that. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text focuses on 'grouped by severity ... in the posted PR comment', and grouping defects are detectable before network posting.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline comment-generation path with mixed severities and assert generated PR comment body has distinct error/warning/info sections with correct membership/counts; use a fake comment publisher to inspect payload.

behaviors.description: A pull request touching 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute GitHub Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timeout behavior depends on real subprocess/runtime performance and CI environment constraints. MOCK-HIDE: mocked linter or fake timing would hide true throughput/regression failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT TIE-BREAKER (Integration vs System): concrete higher-level-only failure is process startup/I/O contention causing wall-clock overrun in actual runner; in-process fakes cannot reproduce this. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because no AI/ML output dependence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in CI on a synthetic PR with ~200 changed files and real linter invocation; measure wall-clock duration and assert completion plus comment posting occurs under 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: If the linter process crashes on a malformed file, remaining files are still analyzed and results are posted.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says 'linter process crashes', which is subprocess/OS-level failure handling. MOCK-HIDE: replacing crash with a normal exception can miss real exit-code/signal handling bugs and worker recovery behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT TIE-BREAKER (Integration vs System): concrete higher-only failure is supervisor misinterpreting real crash exit status and aborting whole run. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with real linter subprocess against a malformed file that triggers an actual crash/non-zero termination; assert orchestration continues analyzing remaining files and still publishes partial findings.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can be reproduced within one local dedup function/object given findings list input; no module boundary is required. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: no external dependency needed; deterministic local inputs expose bug directly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text 'Duplicate findings on the same line ... are deduplicated' is algorithmic output normalization and does not require cross-module or infra interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Call deduplication routine with findings containing same file/line/rule-overlap cases and assert output keeps one canonical finding per dedup key while preserving distinct non-duplicate findings.
